summary: Implemented and validated an ABI-preserving runtime-guarded fast path. The guard uses checked integer-address ranges under the recorded flat-uintptr x86-64 policy, dominates bound caching, and routes uncertain/overlapping cases to the original pointer-loaded loop. No commit was created.

alternatives.reason: The guarded candidate passed correctness gates and showed measured improvement for all five material benchmark sizes.
The public FFI boundary has uncertain external callers, and an intentional overlapping caller exists.
It changes behavior when output writes modify the pointer-loaded bound.
Checked runtime enforcement safely supports caching only on the fast path while preserving the original fallback.
The runtime guard preserves the existing ABI without broader API changes.
All required correctness, sanitizer, compiler-record, performance, and evidence-linkage gates passed.

commands.outcome: Passed repeatedly with matching semantic digests.
Original untouched build and 1/1 original tests passed; final build and 2/2 tests passed.
Passed all 11 retained validation commands, both for the staged digest-linked candidate and final source.
Production kernel and differential validation compiled cleanly; differential execution passed.

validation: Generated defined-behavior differential corpus: 1,127 executions, including zero, negative, VF=8 boundaries, scalar tails, every positive count 1–257, bound before/inside/end/after output, overlap in both directions, 16 aligned layouts, and two checked uintptr boundary refusals.
Explicit witnesses observed 1,123 fast-path and 4 fallback executions.
ASan/UBSan repeated the full 1,127-execution corpus cleanly; the production wrapper test also passed under both sanitizers.
Structured LLVM YAML observed the exact fast loop as Vectorized with VF=8/IC=4 and the exact fallback loop as UnsupportedUncountableLoop plus MissedDetails.

Seeded benchmark used eight sizes, seven warmups, randomized paired ordering, and 31 raw samples per size. All five sizes from 257–65,536 passed the three-times-summed-MAD separation rule; linked material-size median ratio was 8.20×.
Final src/kernel.c SHA-256 is 8e6490d23439e1f2b362bc24799443a9b4549557b7da94bdf4f538429431d5cf, matching the candidate and linked validation/action trace.
evidence_language: observed: Clang 21 missed the original loop as uncountable under the recorded configuration.
tested sufficient assumption: isolated parameter.count.noalias changed the matched loop to vectorized; this is not asserted as a caller contract.
validated on covered executions: the guarded candidate matched the original pointer-loaded implementation across the stated corpus and sanitizer executions.
observed: the final fast path vectorized and the preserved fallback remained missed in structured optimization records.
residual_risks: Covered executions do not establish formal semantic equivalence.
The integer-address guard is intentionally limited to the recorded flat_uintptr_x86_64 policy.
External FFI callers remain uncertain, which is why no global restrict promise was introduced.
Benchmark results apply to the recorded machine, compiler, flags, and zero-addend overflow-safe workload; the eight-element case showed no material improvement.
